commands.review_summary:
The PR routes shared manual `/approve` submissions through the approval gateway resolver, updates focused approval-command tests, and adjusts slash-command docs plus the changelog for plugin approvals.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main sends manual approvals through `callGateway`, while the existing resolver and gateway visibility rules show plugin approvals need the operator approvals runtime path. I did not run the live Slack failure locally; the PR body provides after-fix real-Gateway proof.
commands.review_followup: The PR has a protected `maintainer` label and a compatibility decision about the approval submission path; there is no narrow ClawSweeper repair to queue.

target.title: fix(approval): resolve /approve plugin ids through approvals client
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: manual `/approve` commands used the raw gateway request path, so the request could use the wrong approval-client identity/path for active plugin approvals and report a live `plugin:<id>` approval as `unknown or expired`.
- Solution: route shared `/approve` submissions through the approval resolver, which connects with the operator approvals client path and selects `plugin.approval.resolve` for plugin ids while preserving the existing exec behavior.
- What changed: updated the shared approve command handler, refreshed focused tests around resolver calls and plugin routing, and documented that `/approve` handles exec and plugin approvals.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): approval authorization policy, approver config shape, native approval delivery, and approval id formats are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Any channel that exposes the shared `/approve` command needs manual `plugin:<id>` approvals to reach the same trusted approval runtime as native approval actions. The old raw gateway call path could miss active plugin approvals and return `unknown or expired approval id`.
- Slack is the channel where the regression was observed and where the live proof was exercised, but the code path is the shared command handler used by channel command surfaces, not a Slack-only fix.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: manual channel `/approve plugin:<id>` should resolve pending plugin approvals instead of returning `unknown or expired approval id`.
- Real environment tested: isolated local OpenClaw Gateway on macOS/Darwin arm64, Node v24.15.0. The proof used a Slack native command context against the real Gateway approval APIs and resolver path; it did not call the Slack network.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module <temporary proof harness> | tee /tmp/openclaw-pr-84678-approval-proof.txt`; then `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
proof started gateway_url=ws://127.0.0.1:52702
proof command_context=slack native /approve
proof approval_listener=ready scopes=operator.approvals internal_runtime=true
plugin request accepted status=accepted id=plugin:fe8734e7-2b24-4549-9bc3-6e9a675c0427
plugin command reply=✅ Approval allow-once submitted for plugin:fe8734e7-2b24-4549-9bc3-6e9a675c0427.
plugin waitDecision id=plugin:fe8734e7-2b24-4549-9bc3-6e9a675c0427 decision=allow-once
exec request accepted status=accepted id=proof-exec-3a13b093-ef6e-48cb-a09e-827bd7756e66
exec command reply=✅ Approval allow-once submitted for proof-exec-3a13b093-ef6e-48cb-a09e-827bd7756e66.
exec waitDecision id=proof-exec-3a13b093-ef6e-48cb-a09e-827bd7756e66 decision=allow-once
gateway events=plugin.approval.requested,plugin.approval.resolved,exec.approval.requested,exec.approval.resolved
proof result=pass
```

Focused test output:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```

- Observed result after fix: `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once` in a channel command context resolved a live pending plugin approval through `plugin.approval.resolve`, and `/approve <exec-id> allow-once` still resolved an exec approval through `exec.approval.resolve`. Gateway events included requested and resolved events for both approval families.
- What was not tested: actual Slack app network slash-command callback delivery from Slack's servers; this proof invokes the Slack command handler locally while using a real Gateway and WebSocket approval clients.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): prior manual Slack `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once` returned `Failed to submit approval: unknown or expired approval id`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the `/approve` command bypassed the approval resolver and used a raw gateway call. For plugin approvals, that raw path could use the wrong approval-client identity/path and miss the plugin approval manager that owned the in-memory request.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests asserted raw gateway method calls instead of verifying the same resolver path used by native approval actions.
- Contributing context (if known): plugin approvals and exec approvals have different backing managers, and plugin ids need explicit plugin resolution instead of a raw default gateway resolve attempt.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once` calls the trusted approval resolver with plugin routing and still enforces configured approvers across the shared command path.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression was in shared command routing, before any live channel delivery behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing approve-command tests covered parsing/auth and are updated to assert the resolver path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Manual `/approve` commands now support plugin approval prompts across channel command surfaces as documented, instead of being limited to exec approvals.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
/channel /approve plugin:<id> -> raw gateway resolve -> plugin approval may be missed -> unknown/expired

After:
/channel /approve plugin:<id> -> approval runtime resolver -> operator approvals client path -> plugin approval manager -> resolved
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Darwin 25.2.0 arm64
- Runtime/container: local worktree, Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): shared manual `/approve` command path; verified with a Slack command context and focused command-handler tests
- Relevant config (redacted): channel plugin approval approver config exercised in tests

### Steps

1. Submit `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once` from a configured approver.
2. Handler authorizes the sender for plugin approval.
3. Handler calls `resolveApprovalOverGateway` with plugin routing.

### Expected

- Pending plugin approval resolves or the actual resolver error is returned.

### Actual

- Focused tests pass and assert plugin approval routing through the resolver.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real local Gateway proof for channel-context `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once`, real local Gateway proof for exec `/approve <id> allow-once`, and focused approve-command test suite with exec approvals, plugin approvals, fallback routing, authorization, and gateway-client scopes.
- Edge cases checked: unauthorized plugin approvers remain rejected in tests; legacy unprefixed fallback still attempts exec then plugin in tests; plugin-only not-found errors surface cleanly in tests.
- What you did **not** verify: actual Slack network callback from the hosted Slack app in this split worktree.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: changing the manual command path could regress exec approval submission.
  - Mitigation: focused tests assert exec approval routing still submits successfully and preserve existing fallback behavior.
target.author: kevinslin
